title: Movement strategies shape wolf habitat selection in human-dominated Central European landscapes
author: anonymous
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Code and Data Repository: Movement strategies shape wolf habitat selection in human-dominated Central European landscapes  
 Overview 
 This repository contains the R and shell scripts, along with the corresponding datasets, required to reproduce the integrated Step Selection Analysis (iSSA) and the subsequent post-hoc evaluations of animal movement distributions presented in the manuscript. The code demonstrates the pipeline from model fitting (using conditional logistic regression approximated via Poisson formulation) to the extraction of updated movement parameters. 
 Directory Structure and File Descriptions 
 1. Data Files (`.RData`) 
 These files contain the annotated locations, generated random steps, and fitted model objects necessary for the reproducible workflow: 
 
  new_dat_issa_n50.RData : The main input dataset containing annotated GPS tracking data (cases) and generated random steps (controls) required for the iSSA. It includes movement strategies, extracted environmental covariates (e.g., land cover, distances to roads), and standard movement metrics (step lengths, turning angles). It is filtered for 50 control steps per observed step. 
  n50_iSSA_final_all.RData`, `n50_iSSA_final_res.RData`, `n50_iSSA_final_dsp.RData`, `n50_iSSA_final_flt.RData : Direct outputs from the computationally intensive model fitting process (script `09b`). Each file contains a fitted `glmmTMB` Poisson model object (`model_result`) for a specific data subset: all individuals combined, residents, dispersers, and floaters, respectively. 
 
 2. Analytical Scripts The scripts are numbered sequentially to represent the analytical pipeline: 
 
  09a_iSSA.sh : A shell script (PBS batch) for submitting the computationally intensive model fitting process to a high-performance computing (HPC) cluster. 
  09b_iSSA.R:  The core modelling script. It fits the hierarchical iSSA models (via Poisson approximation in `glmmTMB`) with 22 random effects across different movement strategy datasets. 
  10_coef_var.R : Extracts coefficients and random effect variances from the fitted models. Performs linear hypothesis tests (e.g., interaction testing for Day vs. Night differences) using the `multcomp` package and generates comparative coefficient plots. 
  11_movement_distribution.R : Updates the tentative step-length distributions (Gamma) based on the fitted iSSA model coefficients. Generates density visualisations contrasting movement speeds/distances between day and night. 
  12_displacement_turnangle.R : Calculates the expected short-scale displacement using the Delta method to propagate variance from the model. Updates turning angle distributions (von Mises) and generates their confidence intervals via multivariate normal simulations
